observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the correct depreciation expenses of its assets and a journal entry must be drawn to update/correct the accumulated depreciation by a debit to depreciation expense (for the current year depreciation), debit to prior year’s adjustment (for the prior year’s depreciation) and a credit to accumulated depreciation (for the total unrecorded depreciation).  

Exert effort through the General Services Office, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation for those depreciable assets not provided with depreciation.

Create an appraisal committee to conduct evaluation of assets whose records could not be found in order to establish the commencement of depreciation of the subjected assets and determine their depreciable cost and estimated remaining life for the computation of their respective depreciation.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all depreciable properties owned by the LGU are provided with depreciation in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations to ensure its fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in this year’s report.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement the programmed projects and close monitoring be made to make sure that they are implemented within the planned timeframe.

We recommended that management (i) minimize or stop the practice of serving meals and snacks for entertaining guests or visitors in the normal course of conducting official functions and during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the incurrence of irregular an unnecessary expenditures and wastage of government funds; (ii) ensure that expenses to be reimbursed pass the “public purpose test” by requiring that the basic supporting documents for government expenditures are attached to the claims; and (iii) explain why these transactions will not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the SWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following:
Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution which should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety.

We recommended that MRFs be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays. We recommended that management stop charges expenses such as payment of Electricity, Internet and Telephone bills, purchase of office supplies, travel expenses in attending meetings, submitting reports and others which are not included in the activities enumerated under Section 1 of the Republic Act No. 5447 and strictly limit the same only to those enumerated thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer theunutilized appropriation for LDRRMF to special trust so as to avoid any possible misuse of the fund.

We also recommended prioritize the implementation of projects and activities under the LDRRM.
We recommended that Municipal Accountant submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required by Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management stop inequitable allocation of financial aid or assistance as cash incentive bonus of job order employees of the municipality.  The Sangguniang Bayan must formulate implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation that  harmonize with the Administrative Order No. 5 series of 2008 of the DSWD and at the same time, to avoid the inequitable allocation of financial aid or assistance.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement/prioritize implementation and close monitoring of all the municipal projects to make sure that they are implemented within the planned timeframe.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board pass a resolution requiring every household to have a receptacle for the segregation of solid waste pending collections. Also, we recommend management provide appropriation for its Ecological Solid Waste Management, create a Solid Waste Management Board, develop the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction:
Management has submitted a 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan but failed to comply programs incorporated in the said plan.

Audit observation and recommendation are reiterated in part 2 of this report.
